content.text: Profile: Indian Cricketer & Captain of the Indian women’s cricket team in Tests and ODIs.
Records: Highest run-scorer in Women’s International Cricket and first woman to cross 6,000 runs in ODIs.
Profile: A police officer and Kabaddi champion known for her raiding skills.
Struggle: Sold flowers to support her family while practicing at Kanteerava Stadium.
Achievement: Won a Gold Medal in Kabaddi at the age of 29.
Profile: One of the top shuttlers in the world; Silver medalist in the 2016 Olympics.
Awards: Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna (2016), Padma Shri (2015), Arjuna Award (2013).
Dedication: Travelled 56 km daily to reach Pullela Gopichand’s Academy.
Women's Cricket World Cup: First held in England in 1973.
Kabaddi Dominance: India won all six Women's Kabaddi World Cups (2012-2017).

quiz.question: Who is known as the 'Tendulkar of Indian Women’s Cricket'?
quiz.explanation: Mithali Raj is called the Tendulkar of Indian women’s cricket due to her incredible scoring records.

quiz.question: Mithali Raj's mother tongue is _________.
quiz.explanation: Mithali Raj was born in a Tamil family even though she lives in Hyderabad.

quiz.question: The first Women's Cricket World Cup was held in which country?
quiz.explanation: The first Women's Cricket World Cup was held in England in 1973.

quiz.question: P.V. Sindhu started playing badminton at the age of _________.
quiz.explanation: P.V. Sindhu started playing badminton at the age of 8.

quiz.question: Usha Rani sold _________ to support her family before becoming a Kabaddi star.
quiz.explanation: Usha Rani sold flowers outside temples to support her family while practicing Kabaddi.

quiz.question: Which award did Mithali Raj receive from the Government of India?
quiz.explanation: Mithali Raj was conferred with the Padma Shri award.

quiz.question: India won the Women's Kabaddi World Cup how many times between 2012 and 2017?
quiz.explanation: India won all six Women's Kabaddi World Cups held during that period.

quiz.question: P.V. Sindhu won which medal in the 2016 Rio Olympics?
quiz.explanation: P.V. Sindhu won the Silver medal in the 2016 Olympics.

quiz.question: Who was P.V. Sindhu's coach and inspiration?
quiz.explanation: She was inspired by Pullela Gopichand and joined his academy.

quiz.question: Usha Rani is currently serving in which police force?
quiz.explanation: She was selected for the Karnataka State Police Force for her sporting excellence.

quiz.question: Mithali Raj was selected for the Indian team at the age of _________.
quiz.explanation: She made her international debut at the age of 17.

quiz.question: What is the correct order of adjectives in English grammar?
quiz.explanation: The standard order is Quantity, Quality, Size, Age, and then Colour.

quiz.question: Mithali Raj is the first woman cricketer to surpass _________ runs in ODIs.
quiz.explanation: She was the first woman player to cross the 6,000-run mark in One Day Internationals.

quiz.question: Which of P.V. Sindhu's parents were volleyball players?
quiz.explanation: Both of P.V. Sindhu's parents were professional volleyball players.

quiz.question: Usha Rani won a Gold Medal at the age of _________.
quiz.explanation: She won the Gold Medal when she was 29 years old.

quiz.question: The word 'Consecutive' means _________.
quiz.explanation: Consecutive means following in unbroken or logical sequence.

quiz.question: The second most popular sport in the world is _________.
quiz.explanation: According to the lesson, Soccer (Football) is 1st and Badminton is the 2nd popular sport.

quiz.question: What award did P.V. Sindhu win in 2013?
quiz.explanation: P.V. Sindhu won the Arjuna Award in 2013.

quiz.question: Mithali Raj scored 76 not out in a match against which country in 2018?
quiz.explanation: She scored an unbeaten 76 against South Africa in a T20 match.

quiz.question: Usha Rani is famous for which Kabaddi skill?
quiz.explanation: She is highly acclaimed for her lightning-fast raiding skills.

quiz.question: Identify the adjective in: 'She is a talented cricketer.'
quiz.explanation: 'Talented' describes the noun 'cricketer', so it is an adjective.

quiz.question: How many kilometers did Sindhu travel daily for practice?
quiz.explanation: She traveled 56 km every day from her house to the coaching camp.

quiz.question: P.V. Sindhu was awarded the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna in which year?
quiz.explanation: She received the Khel Ratna award in 2016 after her Olympic success.

quiz.question: The synonym of 'Privilege' is _________.
quiz.explanation: Privilege is a special right or honour given to a person.

quiz.question: Which state does Usha Rani belong to?
quiz.explanation: Ushra Rani is from Bengaluru, Karnataka.

quiz.question: Mithali Raj has scored _________ consecutive half-centuries.
quiz.explanation: Mithali Raj holds the record for 7 consecutive fifties in ODIs.

quiz.question: What does 'Determined' mean?
quiz.explanation: Determined means having made a firm decision and being resolved not to change it.

quiz.question: What is the common error correction for: 'My sister is six feet high.'?
quiz.explanation: When referring to height of humans, we use 'tall' instead of 'high'.

quiz.question: Mithali Raj lives in which city?
quiz.explanation: Although her mother tongue is Tamil, she resides in Hyderabad.

quiz.question: Pullela Gopichand was a champion in which sport?
quiz.explanation: Pullela Gopichand is a former All-England badminton champion and coach.
